title: MLE vs MAP, on a Coin Flip
tagline: Slide the prior, flip some coins, watch the estimate move from MLE toward MAP.
summary: A live Beta-Binomial demo. Choose a Beta(α, β) prior, observe a sequence of flips, see the likelihood, prior, and posterior on the same axis. MLE is where the likelihood peaks; MAP is where the posterior peaks. With few flips the prior dominates; with many flips MAP → MLE. The single clearest picture of how Bayesian inference works on a finite sample.
highlights: Slide α and β to set a Beta prior; the likelihood + prior + posterior redraw live.
Flip 1, 10, 100, or 1000 coins of true bias p; watch how the posterior tightens with N.
Side panel reads off MLE estimate, posterior mean, and KL between posterior and prior.
